title: Commersonia bartramia
intro: Commersonia bartramia, also known as the quandong, is a small tree or shrub native to Western Australia, belonging to the family Malvaceae. Traditionally used by Indigenous Australians, Commersonia bartramia is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to aid digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Commersonia bartramia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoid saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Commersonia bartramia include a jam or paste, known as quandong jam, which is used as a topping or in baking, as well as a tea made from the leaves or fruit of the plant.
taxonomy: Commersonia bartramia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. It is native to Australia and is a member of the genus Commersonia, which is a part of the larger family Malvaceae. The Malvaceae family is a group of flowering plants that includes hibiscus, okra, and cotton, among others. Commersonia bartramia is further classified as a member of the order Malvales, which is a group of flowering plants that are characterized by five-petaled flowers. This classification helps to group Commersonia bartramia with other plants that share similar characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
uses: Commersonia bartramia, a plant native to Australia, has been traditionally used in medicine by Indigenous Australians. The plant's leaves and stems are used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to their medicinal uses. Commersonia bartramia is also believed to have antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and preventing infection. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Commersonia bartramia as a medicinal plant.

properties: Commersonia bartramia, also known as the pink quinine, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its various therapeutic properties.
